title: California Residential Security Deposits
summary: Landlords generally must return deposit balances and itemized deductions within statutory timelines.
key_points: Request move-out inspection records and condition photos.
Ask for itemized deductions with receipts where required.
Track statutory deadlines from the date possession was returned.
If unresolved, evaluate small claims options and demand letter timing.

title: Federal Employment Discrimination Basics
summary: Title VII prohibits discrimination based on protected traits and generally requires EEOC charge processes.
key_points: Preserve timeline of incidents, witnesses, and written communications.
Review internal HR policy and reporting channels.
Watch filing deadlines for agency charge submission.
Retaliation protections may apply after good-faith reporting.

title: Small Claims Process Overview
summary: Small claims courts are designed for lower-dollar disputes with simplified procedure and evidence presentation.
key_points: Confirm jurisdictional amount limits for your court.
Organize exhibits chronologically with copies for the judge and defendant.
Send pre-suit demand where required and keep proof of delivery.
Prepare concise statement of facts and requested relief.

title: New York Tenant Harassment and Repairs
summary: New York tenants may have remedies for harassment and failures to provide habitable conditions.
key_points: Document repair requests in writing and keep copies.
Capture dated photos/videos and inspection reports.
Review local housing court forms and emergency repair channels.
Avoid withholding rent without reviewing local legal requirements.

title: Texas Wage Claim Pathways
summary: Texas workers can pursue certain unpaid wage claims administratively or through civil action depending on facts.
key_points: Collect pay stubs, schedules, and employer communications.
Calculate regular and overtime amounts separately.
Compare agency claim process versus direct lawsuit strategy.
Track limitations periods and retaliation concerns.
